commit.message:
Add environment variable configuration support for agents (#23)

## Summary
This PR adds support for configuring environment variables per agent
through the settings configuration file. Agents can now have environment
variables set when they are executed.

## Key Changes
- Added `env` field to `AgentConfig` struct as an optional
`HashMap<String, String>`
- Updated the default `AgentConfig` implementation to include `env:
None`
- Modified agent execution in `Agent::run()` to apply configured
environment variables to the spawned process
- Added test case `test_parse_settings_with_env()` to verify environment
variable parsing from JSON configuration
- Updated `examples/settings.json` to demonstrate the new `env`
configuration option

## Implementation Details
- The `env` field uses `#[serde(default)]` to maintain backward
compatibility with existing configurations
- Environment variables are applied using `Command::envs()` when
executing an agent, only if the `env` field is present
- The example configuration demonstrates setting `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TURNS`
environment variable for the Claude agent

https://claude.ai/code/session_018Kkvur5pwzKkwKBZr55MtQ

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

- nixpacks configurado para servir assets com 'serve'

commit.message:
Rewrite trailing stop: fully software-managed via ClosePosition

Alpaca does not support stop orders on options (HTTP 422). Rewrote the
trailing stop engine to track price entirely in software and close
positions via ClosePosition when the stop level is hit. Removed all
stop-order placement code, PendingStopLoss type, and MsgStopLossPlaced.

Fix agent routing: strip job context before scoring, raise JT-Entry threshold

The [Context: ...] prefix injected by the frontend was causing false
regex matches (e.g. "update...project" spanning from user message into
context prefix). Now strip context before scoring. Also raised the
JT-Entry override threshold from 0.7 to 0.9 when Know-it-All is
explicitly selected, so only very explicit task operations get rerouted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Per-core IPC, WASM SFI runtime, heterogeneous scheduling, and TLA+ formal verification

- Per-core IPC endpoint pools: eliminate global lock contention, core ID encoded in handle bits
- Cross-core mailbox + IPI: bounded per-core message queues with LAPIC wake signaling
- Network-transparent IPC routing: routing table + 80-byte wire message format ("Swarm OS")
- Heterogeneous compute targets: CPU/GPU/NPU tags in thread model, scheduler filters by target
- WASM interpreter (libs/sotos-wasm): no_std, 50+ opcodes, bounds-checked linear memory (SFI)
- TLA+ formal specs: IPC (no double delivery), capabilities (monotonic rights), scheduler (no starvation)
- CLRS-style LaTeX paper (docs/paper.tex) with TikZ diagrams and algorithm pseudocode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Major portfolio expansion: Sysmon rules, SIEM queries, Linux/macOS, CI/CD, runbooks

Detection Rules (10 new):
- 6 Sysmon rules: process creation, network C2, registry persistence,
  file creation, DNS beaconing, LSASS access (EventIDs 1,3,10,11,13,22)
- 3 Linux rules: SSH brute force, cron persistence, sudo privilege escalation
- 1 macOS rule: LaunchAgent/LaunchDaemon persistence

SIEM Conversion Queries (6 files):
- Splunk SPL: brute force (4 queries), process creation, AWS IAM
- Sentinel KQL: brute force (3 queries), AWS IAM chain detection
- Elastic: KQL threshold rule + EQL sequence detection

Documentation (6 new files):
- INCIDENT_RESPONSE_RUNBOOKS.md: step-by-step runbooks for 7 alert types
- THREAT_HUNTING_PLAYBOOKS.md: 8 hypothesis-driven hunt playbooks with queries
- FALSE_POSITIVE_TUNING.md: real-world FP examples + specific YAML fixes
- ALERT_THRESHOLDS.md: per-rule threshold/aggregation recommendations
- COMPLIANCE_MAPPINGS.md: PCI-DSS, NIST 800-53, CIS Controls, SOC 2 mappings
- ARCHITECTURE.md: end-to-end pipeline diagram + SIEM deployment matrix

CI/CD (.github/workflows/validate.yml):
- YAML lint + Sigma schema validation on every push/PR
- Rule logic test suite execution
- Python code quality (pyflakes, flake8)
- MITRE ATT&CK coverage report on merge to main

Code fixes:
- Moved ipaddress import to module level in log_parser.py
- Added requirements.txt documenting optional dev dependencies
- Updated README to reflect 17-rule project with full skill table

commit.message:
Fix lock leak root cause (session mismatch) + syntax check HTTP 406

Root cause of persistent lock leak: Each adtRequest call creates its own
HTTP session (CSRF token + cookies). Lock runs in Session A, Write in
Session B, Unlock in Session C. SAP rejects the Unlock because Session C
doesn't own the lock from Session A.

Fix: Add per-destination CSRF + cookie session cache (_adtSessionCache).
The first write request (Lock) fetches CSRF + cookies and caches them.
Subsequent write requests (Write, Unlock, Activation) reuse the cached
session. This ensures Lock→Write→Unlock all share the same HTTP session.
Cache expires after 5 minutes. Cleared on 403 (CSRF expired).

Applied to both Strategy 1 (Cloud SDK) and Strategy 2 (raw proxy).

Also fixed syntax_check HTTP 406: Accept header was
"application/vnd.sap.adt.checkobjects+xml" but SAP returns
"Accepted content types: application/vnd.sap.adt.checkmessages+xml".
Fixed Accept to "application/vnd.sap.adt.checkmessages+xml".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent false OVERDUE classification for ML CE orders

_ml_was_late() now uses first_shipped_at (DB trigger) for CE orders
instead of falling back to urgency==OVERDUE, which was incorrectly
set for shipped orders past their deadline regardless of when shipped.

_refresh_delivered_ml_orders() now corrects urgency to 'on_time' when
a CE order is confirmed delivered before its limit_delivery_date.

Also exposes limit_delivery_date in get_shipped_historical() to enable
the urgency correction comparison.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Optimize merge accounts list loading performance

- Check sessionStorage cache before fetching from API
- Reuse comparison data cached by main page (instant load)
- Only fetch from /compare API if cache is missing
- Cache API results in sessionStorage for future use
- Show loading indicator immediately while data loads
- Dramatically improves load time (from seconds to instant when cached)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: cancel in-progress deploy when new push arrives

With cancel-in-progress: false, a stuck or long-running deploy blocks
all subsequent workflow runs — new pushes queue but only one slot is
kept, so they get silently dropped if the in-progress run never finishes.

Switching to cancel-in-progress: true ensures each push immediately
cancels any pending/stuck run and starts fresh. Deployment scripts are
idempotent (services stopped before update, git pull before build) so
mid-run cancellation is safe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(autodev): handle markdown-wrapped JSON in parse_analysis and parse_review (#199)

* fix(autodev): handle markdown-wrapped JSON in parse_analysis and parse_review

- Add extract_json_from_text() to extract JSON from ```json code blocks
- Update parse_analysis() with 4-tier fallback: envelope→direct, envelope→markdown, raw, raw→markdown
- Update parse_review() with same markdown extraction fallback
- Trim whitespace in parse_output/parse_analysis/parse_review
- Add tests for all new parsing paths

Closes #196
Closes #197

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(autodev): extract generic try_parse_with_fallbacks for parse_analysis/parse_review

Unify duplicated 4-tier fallback logic into a single generic function,
reducing ~60 lines of copy-paste between parse_analysis and parse_review.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(autodev): use relaxed epsilon in float comparison test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(autodev): update Cargo.lock version to 0.9.12

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(autodev): reuse try_parse_with_fallbacks and harden json marker matching

- Make try_parse_with_fallbacks pub(crate) for crate-wide reuse
- Replace duplicated envelope+direct parse in parse_knowledge_suggestion
  and daily.rs with try_parse_with_fallbacks (gains markdown fallback)
- Guard extract_json_from_text against ```jsonl/```json5 false matches
- Add tests for jsonl/json5 marker rejection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(autodev): simplify json marker guard with is_ascii_alphanumeric

Replace explicit whitespace char list with is_ascii_alphanumeric() check
to reject ```jsonl/```json5 while accepting any non-alphanumeric separator.

commit.message:
Remove lxml dependency for Vercel: use built-in html.parser only

Switch to html.parser (built-in Python) with no dependencies. Single parse
optimization still works, just slightly slower parsing but compatible everywhere.

fix(auth): TOKEN_REFRESHED não recarrega dados; CheckIn recupera token expirado

AppContext: evento TOKEN_REFRESHED agora apenas atualiza session/user,
sem chamar loadAllData() — impede setLoading(true) que causava blink/desmonte.

CheckIn: se session.access_token for nulo (token expirado ou sessão perdida),
tenta refreshSession() de forma segura (não causa mais blink) antes de
mostrar o card de sessão expirada.

commit.message:
feat(voice): add raw PCM WebSocket format to FastRTC backend

Add audio_format parameter to FastRTCVoiceBackend:
- "mulaw" (default): mu-law encoded audio in JSON (Twilio-compatible)
- "pcm": raw PCM-16 LE binary WebSocket frames

PCM mode sends binary frames via send_bytes() instead of JSON,
avoiding mu-law encoding and base64 overhead. Useful for browser
clients using Web Audio API that can handle PCM directly.
